title: Methods, systems and computer readable media for providing service capability exposure function (scef) as a diameter routing agent(dra) feature
abstract: A system for providing a service capability exposure function (SCEF) as a Diameter routing agent (DRA) feature includes a DRA including a plurality of message processors. The system further includes a cellular device connectivity (CDC) service module operatively associated with the DRA for providing SCEF services as a feature of the DRA, the SCEF services including determining whether to invoke Internet of things (loT) service handling based on content of received messages and for forwarding messages requiring loT service to a service capability server (SCS) or an application server (AS) for receiving the loT service handling.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1 . A system for providing a service capability exposure function (SCEF) as a Diameter routing agent (DRA) feature, the system comprising: a DRA including a plurality of message processors; and a cellular device connectivity (CDC) service module operatively associated with the DRA for providing SCEF services as a feature of the DRA, the SCEF services including determining whether to invoke Internet of things (loT) service handling based on content of received messages and for forwarding messages requiring loT service to a service capability server (SCS) or an application server (AS) for receiving the loT service handling. 2. The system of claim 1 wherein the CDC service module is implemented as a cloud-deployed service and wherein the DRA includes a CDC service interface for accessing the CDC service module transparently to a user of the DRA. 3. The system of claim 2 wherein the SCS and the AS are cloud-based services and the CDC service interface accesses the SCS and AS transparently to the user of the DRA. 4. The system of claim 2 wherein the CDC service interface provides a Diameter interface to the CDC services module. 5. The system of claim 2 wherein the CDC service interface provides a web services interface to the CDC service module. 6. The system of claim 1 wherein the CDC service module is implemented on one of the message processors of the DRA. 7. The system of claim 1 wherein the CDC service module is configured to perform event monitoring for Internet protocol (IP) and non-IP traffic using Diameter signaling with Diameter network nodes. 8. The system of claim 7 wherein the event monitoring includes monitoring location and reachability for IP, non-IP, and SMS- connected devices. 9. The system of claim 1 comprising an loT service module implemented by one of the message processors for providing SCS and AS services for at least some of the received messages. 10. The system of claim 1 comprising a computing platform separate from the DRA and wherein and an loT service module implemented on the computing platform for providing SCS and AS services for at least some of the received messages. 1 1 . A method for providing a service capability exposure function (SCEF) as a Diameter routing agent (DRA) feature, the method comprising: providing a DRA including a plurality of message processors; and providing a cellular device connectivity (CDC) service module operatively associated with the DRA for providing SCEF services as a feature of the DRA, the SCEF services including determining whether to invoke Internet of things (loT) service handling based on content of received messages and for forwarding messages requiring loT service to a service capability server (SCS) or an application server (AS) for receiving the loT service handling. 12. The method of claim 1 1 wherein providing the CDC service module includes providing the CDC service module as a cloud-deployed service and wherein providing the DRA comprises providing a CDC service interface as a feature of the DRA for accessing the CDC service module transparently to a user of the DRA. 13. The method of claim 12 wherein the SCS and the AS are cloud-based services and wherein the method further comprises providing, by the CDC service interface, access to the SCS and AS transparently to the user of the DRA. 

 The method of claim 12 wherein providing the interface to the CDC service module includes providing a Diameter interface to the CDC service module. The method of claim 12 wherein providing the interface to the CDC service module comprises providing a web services interface to the CDC service module. The method of claim 1 1 wherein providing the CDC service module includes implementing the CDC service module on one of the message processors of the DRA. The method of claim 1 1 comprising perform event monitoring for Internet protocol (IP) and non-IP traffic using Diameter signaling with Diameter network nodes. The method of claim 17 wherein the event monitoring includes monitoring location and reachability for IP, non-IP, and SMS- connected devices. The method of claim 1 1 comprising providing an loT service module implemented by one of the message processors or by a computing platform separate from the DRA for providing SCS and AS services for at least some of the received messages. A non-transitory computer readable medium having stored thereon executable instructions that when executed by the processor of a computer control the computer to perform steps comprising: providing a DRA including a plurality of message processors; and providing a cellular device connectivity (CDC) service module operatively associated with the DRA for providing SCEF services as a feature of the DRA, the SCEF services including determining whether to invoke Internet of things (loT) service handling based on content of received messages and for forwarding messages requiring loT 

 service to a service capability server (SCS) or an application server (AS) for receiving the loT service handling.
